cards.name: Captain
cards.desc: The head of every Nanotrasen space station who spends most of their shift delegating and protecting the disk.

cards.name: Renault
cards.desc: The Captain's trusty pet fox who spends most shifts walking around aimlessly in the Captain's Office.

cards.name: Chief Engineer
cards.desc: The head of the engineering department and the one who ensures the station runs smoothly. Most of them fail at this.

cards.name: Head of Personnel
cards.desc: Never far far from their closest pal, Ian, they defend the ability to give out AA to anyone who passes.

cards.name: Research Director
cards.desc: The head of the science department who has to wrangle their explosion prone workers around.

cards.name: Head of Security
cards.desc: The head of the security department, charged with putting away Syndicate and Assistant scum alike.

cards.name: Chief Medical Officer
cards.desc: The head of the medical department who makes sure their department isn't committing any intentional or unintentional malpractice.

cards.name: Quartermaster
cards.desc: The head of the supply department that makes sure everyone gets their crates and their ores.

cards.name: Atmospheric Technician
cards.desc: The workers of the Engineering department who ensure the air is breathable and devoid of anything harmful.

cards.name: Station Engineer
cards.desc: The main working arm of the Engineering department, Station Engineers keep the hull and all the machines within intact.

cards.name: Poly
cards.desc: An expert in quantum cracker theory, she continues to scare enginee- INTEGRITY FAILING!
cards.effect: Once per turn, select a card's attack, defense, or effect. Copy that onto this card until the end of your turn. You cannot use this effect until the end of your next turn.

cards.name: Medical Doctor
cards.desc: Medical Doctors are able to cure almost any ailment that may be inflicted on a worker... while also inflicting some themself.

cards.name: Chemist
cards.desc: Chemists are definitely not producing meth instead of life-saving chemicals.

cards.name: Paramedic
cards.desc: The Medical Department's first response member responsible for stabilizing and rescuing Nanotrasen employees.
cards.effect: Once per turn, discard a card from your hand and add one Unit card from your Morgue to your hand.

cards.name: Virologist
cards.desc: The virologist is the person you go crying to that you either have the common cold or a disease that kills you in a few minutes. They might have been the cause of both of them.

cards.name: Coroner
cards.desc: Overseer of the morgue, the coroner ensures that dead bodies are properly stored and have their autopsies recorded.
cards.effect: Once per turn, discard a card from your hand and add one Item card from your Morgue to your hand.

cards.name: Psychologist
cards.desc: Hired to offer emotional reassurance to employees aboard Nanotrasen stations but is usually found secretly dealing their drugs to anyone who asks.

cards.name: Runtime
cards.desc: GCAT. Somehow, the four letters used in genomes can spell cat. How purr-ious.

cards.name: Geneticist
cards.desc: Tasked with manipulating humanoid DNA, the Geneticist is able to give out genetic powers to their fellow crew.

cards.name: Roboticist
cards.desc: The maintainer of all things cyborg, Roboticists build and maintain cyborgs for the station and are able to build a wide variety of exosuits.

cards.name: Scientist
cards.desc: A Scientist's job ranges from manning the R&D desk, to mixing chemicals, to setting up tank transfer valve bombs

cards.name: Xenobiologist
cards.desc: Caretakers of captive slimes, the Xenobiologist breeds many different types of slimes to get unqiue slime extracts, as well as using them to revitalize organs from fauna

cards.name: E-N
cards.desc: Robotics' adorable and lovable clone of Ian, he's got a heart of gold and can even do your taxes for you! Beware any unexpected sparking though.
cards.effect: Can be used to Special Summon a card with 'Cyborg' in the name.

cards.name: Warden
cards.desc: Watcher of the armory and the prison wing, the Warden expertly puts down rowdy criminals with Krav Maga.

cards.name: Security Officer
cards.desc: The sworn enemy of any greytider, Security Officers are hired to keep the station in order and free of crime.

cards.name: Detective
cards.desc: Sometimes masquerading as a noir detective, the Detective uses their deductive skills to help Security find suspects of crimes.

cards.name: Sergeant Araneus
cards.desc: The Head of Security's trusty pet spider is known for her violent retaliations torwards mistreatment.

cards.name: Paperwork
cards.desc: The Internal Affair's pet sloth is as mopey as can be and can sometimes get around to eating paperwork. Still slightly faster than a legal case.

cards.name: Botanist
cards.desc: The main ingredient provider for the chef, skilled botanist can produce plants that can fill a crewmember up and kill them just as quickly.

cards.name: Clown
cards.desc: Honk!
cards.effect: When targeted by an enemy attack or effect, flip a coin. If heads, negate that attack or effect.

cards.name: Janitor
cards.desc: The humble Janitor travels the station in search of any messes that need cleaning.

cards.name: Chef
cards.desc: Wielder of CQC within their kitchen, the Chef protects their kitchen from any invaders trying to disrupt the distribution of food to the crew.

cards.name: Bartender
cards.desc: The station's OTHER source of emotional reassurance, the Bartender serves alcohol to anyone who wants it while shooting any disruptive patrons with their shotgun.

cards.name: Chaplain
cards.desc: The spirtual leader of the station, the Chaplain offers unique services due to their connection with religious higher-ups.

cards.name: Librarian
cards.desc: Usually found within their library writing and printing books, the Librarian is able to loan out books full of knowledge. Don't forget to return them!

cards.name: Pete
cards.desc: The chef's tempermental companion, this goat is not afraid to butt heads with you and take a bite out of your local Diona.

cards.name: Cargo Technician
cards.desc: The main workhorse of the Supply department, Cargo Technicians ensure your crates are delivered soon after you order them.

cards.name: Shaft Miner
cards.desc: Pioneers of Lavaland, Shaft Miners brave the horrors in order to bring materials to the station.

cards.name: Forklift
cards.desc: Supply's pet of choice, Forklift has been taught how to carry crates and wouldn't hurt a fly!

cards.name: Brad
cards.desc: Residing just on the outskirts of the Mining Outpost, Brad is a cockroach that has adapted to the weather patterns of Lavaland and can withstand them.
cards.effect: This card cannot be destroyed by card effects.

cards.name: Assistant
cards.desc: Hired to assist employees around the station, Assistants tend to do the exact opposite of that.

cards.name: Greytide
cards.desc: Born from disgruntled and abused Assistants, the Greytide are a nuisance to Security and the rest of the station alike.
cards.effect: Cannot be Normal Summoned. Send an Assistant from your hand or field to the Morgue to Special Summon this card. When this card is Special Summoned, send one Security Unit to the Morgue.

cards.name: Mouse
cards.desc: The bane of engineers around the sector, the Mouse makes any cables they find a nice meal for themself.
cards.effect: You can Special Summon this card from your hand for no cost.
